question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CommonProperties.getInstance() singleton used in EntityIdRangeParameter.getEntityId() (which provides default shard and realm for 1-part and 2-part entity IDs) to query allowances using just a numeric ID that resolves to a different shard/realm than intended, potentially accessing allowance data for accounts in other network segments?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the approved_for_all boolean field exposed in the NftAllowancesResponse to identify which spender-owner-token combinations grant blanket transfer rights, and then use those approved_for_all=true allowances to transfer all NFTs from a staking account in a single transaction, triggering a full staking reward payout that the attacker can redirect?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any rate limiting or authentication on GET /api/v1/accounts/{id}/allowances/nfts to perform high-frequency enumeration of NFT allowances across thousands of accounts, building a real-time map of which staking accounts have active approved_for_all grants and correlating with pending_reward data from the accounts API to identify the highest-value theft targets?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timestamp field in the NftAllowancesResponse (mapped from timestampRange via NftAllowanceMapper) to identify allowances that were granted just before a staking period boundary, indicating that the owner was actively staking at the time of the grant, and use this to prioritize which allowances to exercise to capture the maximum pending staking reward?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ImmutableSortedMap used in EXTRACTORS (which sorts keys alphabetically, placing account.id before token.id) to predict the exact format of pagination next links, and craft pre-computed next-link URLs that skip directly to allowance records for specific high-value staking accounts without iterating through all preceding pages?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Positive constraint on the limit parameter (which rejects 0 and negative values but allows 1 through MAX_LIMIT=100) by setting limit=1 to enumerate allowances one at a time with minimal server load, performing a slow but undetectable reconnaissance of all NFT allowances for staking accounts to avoid triggering any rate-limit defenses?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Max(MAX_LIMIT) constraint (MAX_LIMIT=100) combined with the absence of a minimum page size to perform binary search enumeration of NFT allowances by varying limit values, efficiently locating the exact position of a target spender's allowance record within the sorted allowance table for a staking account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bound.hasEqualBounds() check (which returns true when adjustedLower == adjustedUpper) combined with adjustLowerRange() rewriting to EQ to cause the service to issue a precise point-lookup query for a specific spender-token pair, confirming whether a specific account has granted an NFT allowance to a known spender without enumerating all allowances?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bound.toLower() and toUpper() methods (which create new Bound instances with only one range parameter) to craft requests that exercise different code paths in the NftAllowanceRepository.findAll() method, potentially triggering query plan differences that leak information about the distribution of allowances for staking accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdParameter.valueOf() fallback chain (first tries EntityIdNumParameter, then EntityIdEvmAddressParameter, then EntityIdAliasParameter) by supplying an ambiguous ID string that could be parsed as multiple formats, causing the controller to resolve to a different account than intended and returning allowance data for an unintended staking account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NftAllowanceServiceImpl.entityService.lookup() call (which resolves the EntityIdParameter to a concrete account ID) by supplying an EVM address that maps to a contract account that also holds staking rewards, enumerating its NFT allowances to discover spenders who can trigger reward payouts from that contract?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any ownership verification in getNftAllowances() (no check that the requester owns or is the spender for the queried account) to enumerate NFT allowances for any account on the network, including accounts with large pending staking rewards, without any authorization challenge?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NftAllowancesResponse structure (which includes both allowances list and links.next) to determine the total count of NFT allowances for a staking account by counting pages, and use this count to infer the account's NFT activity level and likelihood of having large pending staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the field variable assignment (var field = owner ? NFT_ALLOWANCE.SPENDER : NFT_ALLOWANCE.OWNER) to perform a cross-reference attack by querying the same account with both owner=true and owner=false, reconstructing the complete bipartite graph of owner-spender relationships and identifying all accounts that can trigger staking reward payouts for a target staking account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bound constructor's cardinality tracking (EnumMap<RangeOperator, Integer>) by supplying the maximum allowed 2 account.id parameters both with GTE operator, causing verifySingleOccurrence() to throw an exception whose error message reveals internal parameter names and validation logic that aids in crafting more precise allowance enumeration attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the error message from checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity() (which reveals 'token.id parameter must have account.id present', 'Requires the presence of an lte or eq account.id parameter', etc.) to fingerprint the exact validation logic and craft requests that pass all checks while still returning maximum allowance data for staking account reconnaissance?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PageRequest.of(0, limit, sort) construction (which always uses page 0) combined with the cursor-based pagination in linkFactory to bypass any server-side page tracking, allowing the attacker to replay or reorder pagination requests to re-enumerate allowances that may have changed between requests, detecting newly granted allowances on staking accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NftAllowance model's getSpender() and getOwner() methods (used in EXTRACTORS) to infer the internal numeric representation of account IDs from the pagination links, and use these numeric IDs to correlate with staking reward data from other APIs to identify accounts with the highest pending_reward values that have active NFT allowances?
